info.title: Orient-advertise-backend API
info.description:
Multi-module Spring Boot platform for TV-box content delivery — device management, content uploads + transcoding, playlists, scheduling, remote actions, incident lifecycle, proof-of-play, aggregated reporting, Excel export, and external partner integrations.

**Authentication.** Most endpoints require a JWT access token (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`) obtained from `POST /auth/login`. External integration endpoints under `/service/external/**` use an `X-API-Key` header instead — see the *External* group.

**Sensitive endpoints.** Operations marked with `[SENSITIVE]` in their summary affect production state, expose user data, or irreversibly delete records. Audit logging is enabled and the operator's username is recorded.

tags.name: Sensitive
tags.description: Operations that mutate production state or expose protected data — audit logged

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authenticated self-service: change password, set recovery email

tags.name: External
tags.description: Partner integration endpoints — X-API-Key auth, 100 req/hr per key

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: ADMIN-only user lifecycle and advertiser-content linking

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Public password recovery: forgot / reset / validate

tags.name: Sync Groups
tags.description: SyncGroup CRUD and membership (synchronized-playback sales points)

tags.name: Devices
tags.description: Device group CRUD and bulk actions

get.summary: Sync group detail (with member device list)

404.description: Unknown id, or out of operator scope
Unknown id, or out of operator scope

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, name) for the new name

404.description: Unknown id, or out of operator scope

409.description: Group still has active member devices

put.description: Both fields are optional — submit only the one you want to change. A request body with both null is a no-op and returns the unchanged detail.

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, code)

delete.description: Two sequential 409 guards: active devices, then facilities. Regions are not soft-deleted — once a region passes every guard, the row is removed permanently.

409.description: Region still has active devices or facilities

get.summary: [SENSITIVE] List operators assigned to a project

put.summary: [SENSITIVE] Set the whole operator set for a project (bulk add/remove)

409.description: A userId is not an OPERATOR

get.summary: Project detail (with immediate child regions and device groups)

409.description: Duplicate name

delete.description: Refuses with 409 if any region or device group still belongs to this project (two sequential guards: regions, then device groups). Operators must remove the project's regions (with their facilities / devices) and its device groups first. Once empty, the row is removed permanently — projects are not soft-deleted.

409.description: Project still has regions or device groups
Duplicate (project_id, name) for the new name
Playlist is in use by N active assignment(s)

put.summary: Move an item to a new position (sentinel-pattern atomic shift)
Set or clear an item's duration override
put.description: Body: { durationSeconds: int|null }. Non-null sets the override. Null clears it so device-side /playlist falls back to the file's natural duration (content_file.duration_seconds).

404.description: Playlist or item not found / soft-deleted

put.summary: Bulk-reorder items by an exact ordered ID list

200.description: Reordered; returns the new ordered list

400.description: orderedItemIds does not exactly match current item set

put.summary: Set or clear the authenticated user's recovery email
put.description: A blank/null email clears it. The address is used by forgot-password.

409.description: Email already in use by another account

get.summary: Facility detail (with active member device list)

put.summary: Update a facility (rename and/or change address, within its existing region)
put.description: PATCH-style body: every field is independently optional. `null` means "leave unchanged"; non-null but blank is rejected with 400 because the underlying columns reject silent corruption. Cross-region moves are NOT supported here — moving a facility between regions touches device FKs and assignment targets, which are outside the scope of an update. To move a facility, reassign its devices, hard-delete the facility, and create a new one in the target region.

400.description: Validation failed (e.g., blank name or address)

409.description: Duplicate (region_id, name) for the new name

delete.description: Two sequential 409 guards: active devices, then CONFIRMED assignments. Facilities are not soft-deleted — once a facility passes both guards, the row is removed permanently.

409.description: Facility still has active devices, or is targeted by CONFIRMED assignments

get.summary: Device group detail (with member device list)

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, name) for the new name
Group has active devices, or is targeted by CONFIRMED assignments

put.description: Members with no per-device override inherit this on their next heartbeat (no fan-out write — that's the inheritance model). Surfaced on the group detail as `volume`.

404.description: Group missing/soft-deleted, or out of operator scope

delete.description: Members fall back to their per-device override or the default (100) on their next heartbeat.

404.description: Group missing/soft-deleted, or out of operator scope

409.description: Username already exists
Duplicate (project_id, name)

post.summary: Set the members of a sync group
post.description: Sets device.syncGroup to this group for each id. Devices already in this group are no-ops; devices in a different sync group are moved silently and the previous sync-group id is reported in `movedFrom` for audit.

400.description: Devices belong to a different project

404.description: Group out of scope, or one or more devices missing/soft-deleted

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, code)

get.description: Unpaginated by design — projects are a small set in practice (single-digit to low-double-digit). Returns a flat ordered list.

409.description: Duplicate name

post.summary: [SENSITIVE] Assign a single operator to a project

409.description: Duplicate, or user is not an OPERATOR

delete.summary: [SENSITIVE] Unassign an operator from a project (idempotent)

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, name)

post.summary: Add an item to a playlist

400.description: Validation failed; non-READY content; out-of-range position

post.description: Verifies the current password, applies the new one, and revokes ALL sessions. The caller must re-login afterward.

400.description: Wrong current password, mismatch, or policy violation

409.description: Duplicate (region_id, name)

post.summary: [SENSITIVE] Issue a remote action to a device
post.description: Mutates production state on a remote device. Audit-logged; the API key prefix is recorded as the issuer (never the internal id). Bean validation rejects volume out of [0, 100] before reaching the service.

409.description: Pending-action queue full or duplicate type

400.description: Volume out of range, missing type

409.description: Duplicate (project_id, name)

post.description: Sets device.deviceGroup to this group for each id. Devices already in this group are no-ops; devices in a different group are moved silently and the previous group id is reported in `movedFrom` for audit.

400.description: Devices belong to a different project

404.description: Group missing/soft-deleted, or one or more devices missing/soft-deleted

value.message: Urgent upload accepted; transcoding queued at front + WebSocket pushed
Upload accepted as orphan content (no project bound); attach a project via PATCH /api/content/{id}/project. Transcoding in progress

get.summary: Check whether a reset token is still valid
get.description: Non-consuming. Public token oracle, so it is rate-limited the same as the reset POST to blunt brute force (the 256-bit token makes guessing infeasible regardless).

post.summary: Reset a password using a one-time token
Rotate the refresh cookie and mint a fresh access token
post.description: Single-use refresh: the supplied cookie value is invalidated and replaced. Reuse of a previously rotated id is treated as a possible replay and invalidates the entire token family (every refresh token issued under the same login session). Role changes take effect here — the new access token is minted with the user's CURRENT role. The refresh token is read from the `refresh_token` cookie; a fresh cookie is set on the response.

200.description: New access token in body, rotated refresh token in cookie

401.description: Cookie missing, token unknown, expired, or replay detected

post.description: Reads the `refresh_token` cookie, invalidates every refresh token in its family, and clears the cookie on the response. Returns 204 even when the cookie is missing or the token is unknown — no information leak.

post.summary: Authenticate and receive an access token
post.description: Validates credentials, then returns a short-lived JWT access token in the response body and issues a long-lived refresh token as an HttpOnly cookie (`refresh_token`, scoped to `/api/auth`, `SameSite` configurable, default `Strict`). The refresh token is never exposed to JavaScript. Reject reasons: invalid credentials (401), deactivated account (401), no role assigned (401).

401.description: Invalid credentials, deactivated account, or no role assigned

200.description: Authenticated; access token in body, refresh token in `refresh_token` cookie

post.description: If an active account owns the email, a one-time reset link is emailed. The response is identical (202) whether or not the email exists — no account enumeration. Rate-limited per IP and per email.

202.description: Accepted (always, regardless of whether the email exists)

409.description: The playlist has no items. (Time overlap is NOT checked at draft creation — it is enforced device-aware at confirm.)

post.summary: Confirm a draft assignment with optional device exclusions or inclusions
post.description: Send `excludedDeviceIds` to confirm against the full target scope minus those devices. Send `includedDeviceIds` to confirm against ONLY those devices (the backend derives the complement set as exclusions — useful when a target has more than the 200-device preview cap and the FE can't enumerate the full set client-side). The two fields are mutually exclusive: sending both returns 400.

409.description: Time overlap with an existing CONFIRMED assignment (retry with replaceConflicting:true to supersede it — see details.conflicts), the assignment is not in DRAFT status, or the playlist has no items

400.description: Both excludedDeviceIds and includedDeviceIds set, or includedDeviceIds contains ids outside the assignment target scope

get.summary: List API keys (metadata only — no hash, no plaintext)
get.description: Returns prefix + lifecycle metadata. Plaintext keys and SHA-256 hashes are never exposed by this endpoint.

post.description: Returns the plaintext key ONCE in `rawKey`. The database stores only the SHA-256 hash; a lost key cannot be recovered — issue a new one. The 8-char `prefix` is preserved in cleartext for log/UI identification.

patch.summary: Assign or clear the project on a content file

404.description: Unknown content file (or already soft-deleted), or unknown project

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN or OPERATOR

delete.summary: [SENSITIVE] Delete a user (cascades advertiser content access)
delete.description: Removes the user. For ADVERTISERs, every linked content access grant is also removed in the same transaction. Irreversible.

get.description: Aggregates playback for one device over a window: per content file, the play count and total duration in seconds. scope is an object { type, id, name } (type currently always "DEVICE"); the wire carries integer seconds only (the frontend formats H:MM:SS). VIEWER allowed; ADVERTISER → 403; unknown / soft-deleted / out-of-operator-scope device → 404.

Apache POI streaming workbook (`SXSSFWorkbook`) — memory stays bounded regardless of row count. Per-user concurrency is capped at 2; a third concurrent request returns 429. The export aborts cleanly after the 60-second deadline (`X-Export-Timeout-Seconds: 60`). Sheets vary by `type`: EVENTS, DEVICES, or STATS.

400.description: Invalid type, bad ISO dates, or missing facilityId

get.description: Returns counts by type, top affected devices, total incident count, and average resolution time over a date window. For small ranges the report is computed inline (200). For ranges exceeding ~10,000 events the work is queued and a job id is returned (202) — poll the jobs endpoint.
Returns PENDING, COMPLETED, or FAILED. Jobs auto-expire 30 minutes after creation; expired or unknown ids return 404.
Resolves the principal from the JWT and returns the matching active user row.
Returns the device's current operational state, last heartbeat, and active content version. Identifies the device by serial number — internal database ids are never exposed.

parameters.name: serialNumber
parameters.description: Public device serial — never the internal id

get.description: Paginated event log filtered by date range. Page size capped at 100. Default window is the trailing 7 days when `from`/`to` are omitted.

Single-shot aggregation: total devices, status breakdown (ONLINE / OFFLINE / NO_CONTENT), open incidents split by CRITICAL and WARNING, and per-region online vs total counts. Cached for 30 seconds; counts are zero-filled — every status bucket and region appears in the response even when empty, so the frontend renders without null checks.

409.description: Content is referenced by active playlists; remove first

get.summary: Get a presigned URL to stream a content file

403.description: Advertiser caller is not linked to this content

409.description: Content exists but is not READY yet — poll status, retry when READY

delete.summary: Remove a device from this sync group
delete.description: Sets device.syncGroup = null (the device returns to the project's unassigned pool). Removing the last device is allowed; empty sync groups remain valid.

404.description: Group/device missing, or device is not in this group

delete.summary: Remove an item from a playlist (positions compact afterward)

404.description: Playlist/item not found, or item is not in this playlist

delete.summary: Remove a device from this group
delete.description: Sets device.deviceGroup = null. Removing the last device is allowed; empty groups remain valid targets for assignment preview/setup.

404.description: Group/device missing, or device is not in this group

204.description: Cancelled; target devices re-resolve on next sync

delete.description: Marks the key REVOKED. Effective immediately — the next request bearing this key returns 401 because the auth lookup filters on `status=ACTIVE` on every call (no in-memory cache widens the window). Cannot be undone; mint a replacement instead.

409.description: Already revoked

durationOverride.description: Per-item duration override; null means use the source file's natural duration

durationSeconds.description: Duration override in seconds (1..86400); null clears the override so the device falls back to the source file's natural duration

Role.description:
Caller role. Drives @PreAuthorize checks across the API.
- **ADMIN** — full access incl. user/key lifecycle
- **OPERATOR** — device + content operations, no admin lifecycle
- **VIEWER** — read-only dashboards, reports
- **ADVERTISER** — scoped to their granted content files only

addedCount.description: Number of devices newly attached to this sync group (excludes already-member rows)

alreadyMember.description: Device ids the caller asked for that were already in this sync group

items.description: Device ids the caller asked for that were already in this sync group

additionalProperties.description: For devices silently moved out of a different sync group, maps deviceId -> previousSyncGroupId. Empty when no devices were moved.

movedFrom.description: For devices silently moved out of a different sync group, maps deviceId -> previousSyncGroupId. Empty when no devices were moved.

storageKey.description: MinIO object key under the raw bucket

urgent.description: Whether the upload was queued at the front of the transcode pool

projectId.description: Bound project id, or null for orphan uploads

message.example: Urgent upload accepted; transcoding queued at front + WebSocket pushed

DeviceActionType.description:
Remote action a device executes on next pickup. Devices poll
`GET /api/devices/{id}/actions/pending` or receive a WebSocket push.

addedCount.description: Number of devices newly attached to this group (excludes already-member rows)

alreadyMember.description: Device ids the caller asked for that were already attached to this group (no-op)

items.description: Device ids the caller asked for that were already attached to this group (no-op)

additionalProperties.description: For devices silently moved out of a different group, maps deviceId -> previousGroupId. Empty when no devices were moved.

movedFrom.description: For devices silently moved out of a different group, maps deviceId -> previousGroupId. Empty when no devices were moved.

PushResult.description: Result of the DEVICE-facing URGENT_CONTENT fan-out for urgent uploads (null when not urgent or orphan). This is NOT operator transcoding progress — transcoding status (TRANSCODING/READY/FAILED/INVALID) is broadcast separately to operators over the /ws/dashboard WebSocket as CONTENT_STATUS_CHANGE frames.

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT access token. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

AccessTokenResponse.description: Access-token response body. The refresh token is delivered separately as an HttpOnly cookie.

ExportType.description:
Excel export sheet selector for `GET /api/reports/export`.
Each value produces a different sheet and column set.

DeviceImpact.description: Top devices by event count over the window

status.description: PENDING when queued, COMPLETED when inline

jobId.description: Job id; populated only when status=PENDING

totalEvents.description: Total event count over the window — sum of all countsByType entries

additionalProperties.description: Per-event-type counts. Keys are EventType names (e.g. OFFLINE, SYNC_TIMEOUT); values are counts.

countsByType.description: Per-event-type counts. Keys are EventType names (e.g. OFFLINE, SYNC_TIMEOUT); values are counts.

incidentCount.description: Number of incidents derived from the events

avgResolutionSeconds.description: Average incident resolution time in seconds; null when no incidents

topAffectedDevices.description: Top devices by event count over the window

ReportResponse.description: Aggregated event report envelope (inline or queued)

Priority.description:
Event severity. Drives incident routing and the Critical pub/sub fan-out.
- **CRITICAL** — immediate operator attention; pushed via WebSocket
- **HIGH** — should be triaged within minutes
- **MEDIUM** — included in dashboards but not paged
- **LOW** — informational, not surfaced by default
- **INFO** — audit-only, never opens an incident

bearerAuth.description: JWT access token from `POST /auth/login`. Roles encoded in the token: ADMIN, OPERATOR, VIEWER, ADVERTISER.

apiKeyAuth.description: External-integration API key. Issued by ADMIN via `POST /service/admin/service-keys`. Rate-limited 100 req/hr per key. Revocation is immediate — no in-memory cache widens the window.
apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
